steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Set up the tangent ratio
steps.content:
In right triangle XYZ with the right angle at Z, angle X is one of the acute angles. From angle X's perspective:
  • Opposite side: $YZ$
  • Adjacent side: $XZ$
Therefore:
$$\tan X = \frac{YZ}{XZ} = \frac{12}{35}$$

steps.title: Find XZ, then use the Pythagorean triple
steps.content:
Since $YZ = 24$ and $\frac{YZ}{XZ} = \frac{12}{35}$:
$$\frac{24}{XZ} = \frac{12}{35} \implies XZ = \frac{24 \cdot 35}{12} = 70$$
The legs are now in ratio $24: 70 = 12: 35$. This is the 12–35–37 Pythagorean triple. With scale factor $k = 2$, the hypotenuse is $XY = 37 \cdot 2 = 74$.

steps.title: Calculate the perimeter
steps.content:
Adding all three sides:
$$70 + 24 + 74 = 168$$
That is the correct answer.
